name: dsd-neo
description: Modular Digital Speech Decoder fork with SDR, network, protocol, DSP, vocoder integration, tests, packaging, and developer tooling.
build_common_tools_justification: Build uses common tools: CMake, C and C++ compilers, CTest, vcpkg for Windows packaging, and standard platform toolchains.
build_floss_tools_justification: Build and test tools are FLOSS.
build_justification: CMake build and installation are documented in README.md and docs/build-installation.md.
contribution_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents the pull request contribution process.
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md lists DCO, coding, testing, licensing, generated artifact, security, and review requirements.
crypto_call_justification: N/A for product security: DSD-neo's crypto code exists for protocol interoperability and decode support, not as a general-purpose user security boundary. See docs/security-requirements.md.
crypto_floss_justification: Crypto interoperability helpers are implemented in project source distributed under FLOSS terms and documented in THIRD_PARTY.md where applicable.
crypto_keylength_justification: DSD-neo does not choose key lengths for new security mechanisms; radio key lengths are dictated by interoperable protocols and user-supplied material. See docs/security-requirements.md.
crypto_password_storage_justification: DSD-neo does not store passwords for authenticating external users.
crypto_pfs_justification: DSD-neo does not implement key agreement protocols.
crypto_published_justification: Supported radio crypto interoperability paths use published algorithms or protocol-specific vendor formats documented by implementation context; see docs/security-requirements.md.
crypto_random_justification: DSD-neo does not generate cryptographic keys or nonces for users.
crypto_weaknesses_justification: Default product security does not depend on weak cryptography. Legacy DES, RC4, and RC2 support is retained only for interoperable radio decode paths and is documented in docs/security-requirements.md.
crypto_working_justification: Known-weak algorithms are supported only where needed for interoperability with deployed radio protocols or vendor privacy modes, and the risks are documented in docs/security-requirements.md.
delivery_mitm_justification: Repository, release downloads, documentation, and AUR links are distributed over HTTPS.
delivery_unsigned_justification: Release assets are distributed over GitHub HTTPS and tag workflows generate SBOMs and GitHub artifact attestations where supported; see docs/release-verification.md.
description_good_justification: https://github.com/arancormonk/dsd-neo and README.md describe the decoder scope, supported protocols, SDR inputs, network paths, and packaging.
discussion_justification: GitHub Issues, pull requests, and Discussions at https://github.com/arancormonk/dsd-neo/issues, https://github.com/arancormonk/dsd-neo/pulls, and https://github.com/arancormonk/dsd-neo/discussions are searchable and URL-addressable.
documentation_basics_justification: README.md links build, install, CLI, configuration, testing, security, dependency, release, and contribution docs.
documentation_interface_justification: External interfaces are documented in README.md, docs/cli.md, docs/config-system.md, docs/network-audio.md, docs/csv-formats.md, docs/udp-control.md, and public headers under include/dsd-neo/.
dynamic_analysis_enable_assertions_justi: Debug, sanitizer, and fuzz presets keep assertions and runtime diagnostics available for validation builds.
dynamic_analysis_fixed_justification: No confirmed medium-or-higher exploitable dynamic-analysis findings are known open.
dynamic_analysis_justification: Sanitizer CI, TSan preset, and libFuzzer smoke targets are documented in docs/testing.md and tools/fuzz_smoke.sh.
dynamic_analysis_unsafe_justification: C/C++ code is exercised with AddressSanitizer/UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer and fuzz smoke targets.
english_justification: Project documentation and issue discussion are maintained in English.
enhancement_responses_justification: Enhancement requests are tracked and responded to through GitHub Issues, Discussions, and pull requests.
floss_license_justification: LICENSE declares GPL-3.0-or-later for project source.
floss_license_osi_justification: GPL-3.0-or-later is an OSI-approved FLOSS license.
interact_justification: README.md links releases, issues, discussions, support/security reporting, and contribution docs at https://github.com/arancormonk/dsd-neo.
know_common_errors_justification: CI uses C/C++ analyzers, sanitizers, fuzzing, CodeQL, Semgrep, cppcheck, scan-build, GCC fanalyzer, IWYU, lizard, workflow linting, and secret/dependency scanners for common weakness classes.
know_secure_design_justification: docs/security-requirements.md documents threat model, trust boundaries, crypto limitations, and secure design controls.
license_location_justification: License is in LICENSE and third-party notices are in COPYRIGHT, THIRD_PARTY.md, and vendored source paths.
maintained_justification: Project has recent commits, releases, CI, dependency scanning, and active GitHub issue/discussion activity.
no_leaked_credentials_justification: Contributor policy forbids committing credentials, radio keys, private captures, generated outputs, and machine-specific configuration; Gitleaks and GitHub secret scanning run in guardrail checks.
osps_ac_01_01_justification: Sensitive repository resources are hosted on GitHub. Maintainers with sensitive access must keep GitHub multi-factor authentication or passkeys enabled; see docs/openssf-baseline.md.
osps_ac_02_01_justification: New collaborators are added through GitHub repository permissions, with access intentionally granted by the maintainer; see docs/openssf-baseline.md.
osps_ac_03_01_justification: The primary branch is main and GitHub branch protection requires status checks before merge, blocking direct commits outside the protected workflow.
osps_ac_03_02_justification: GitHub branch protection for main has branch deletion disabled.
osps_br_01_01_justification: Workflow policy and Semgrep rules require untrusted GitHub metadata to be passed through action inputs or environment variables instead of direct shell interpolation; see docs/code-quality-guardrails.md, docs/openssf-baseline.md, and semgrep/dsd-neo.yml.
osps_br_01_02_justification: This BadgeApp criterion is obsolete. Current workflows avoid user-controlled branch-name interpolation in shell behavior; protected branch names are configured explicitly where needed.
osps_br_01_03_justification: Pull request workflows use least-privilege read permissions and do not expose privileged publication credentials to untrusted PR code; see docs/openssf-baseline.md.
osps_br_03_01_justification: Official project channels are GitHub HTTPS URLs listed in README.md and docs/openssf-baseline.md.
osps_br_03_02_justification: Official release and source distribution channels are GitHub/AUR HTTPS URLs, protected by authenticated HTTPS transport.
osps_br_07_01_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md forbids committing credentials, radio keys, private captures, generated build outputs, and machine-specific configuration; Gitleaks and GitHub secret scanning run in guardrail checks.
osps_do_01_01_justification: README.md documents build, installation, downloads, CLI examples, input/output paths, runtime tuning, configuration, tests, and project documentation links.
osps_do_02_01_justification: docs/issue-reporting.md documents defect reporting through GitHub Issues.
osps_gv_02_01_justification: GitHub Issues, pull requests, and Discussions are public mechanisms for proposed changes and usage obstacles.
osps_le_02_01_justification: The source code license is GPL-3.0-or-later, an OSI-approved and FSF-free software license.
osps_le_02_02_justification: Released software assets are distributed under GPL-3.0-or-later with retained third-party notices.
osps_le_03_01_justification: The source license is maintained in LICENSE with additional notices in COPYRIGHT and THIRD_PARTY.md.
osps_le_03_02_justification: CMake install rules and packaging workflows include LICENSE, COPYRIGHT, THIRD_PARTY.md, and third-party license texts in release documentation trees.
osps_qa_01_02_justification: The public GitHub git history records changes, authors, and timestamps.
osps_qa_02_01_justification: Direct compiled and tooling dependencies are tracked in CMake files, docs/dependencies.md, vcpkg overlay files, .github/requirements/*.txt, workflow files, and .github/dependabot.yml.
osps_qa_04_01_justification: Not applicable: DSD-neo currently has one authoritative source repository, https://github.com/arancormonk/dsd-neo.
osps_qa_05_01_justification: Contributor policy forbids committing generated build outputs and compiled binaries; generated outputs are ignored under build and vcpkg_installed directories and release assets are published outside the source repository.
osps_qa_05_02_justification: The version control system does not contain generated executable artifacts or unreviewable binary artifacts. Tracked non-source binary assets are limited to reviewable project images and a public upstream attribution PGP key; repository policy forbids generated binaries, private captures, credentials, and radio keys.
osps_vm_02_01_justification: SECURITY.md documents the project security contact path through GitHub private vulnerability reporting.
release_notes_justification: GitHub Releases provide human-readable release notes at https://github.com/arancormonk/dsd-neo/releases.
release_notes_vulns_justification: No publicly known runtime vulnerabilities with CVE assignments were fixed in the latest release.
repo_distributed_justification: Git is a distributed version control system.
repo_interim_justification: The default branch exposes interim development versions between releases.
repo_track_justification: GitHub git history tracks changes, authors, and timestamps.
report_archive_justification: GitHub Issues, Discussions, and pull requests provide archived public history: https://github.com/arancormonk/dsd-neo/issues.
report_responses_justification: Issue reporting policy sets acknowledgement, triage, and response expectations.
sites_https_justification: Official repo, issue tracker, discussion, release, documentation, and AUR URLs use HTTPS.
static_analysis_common_vulnerabilities_j: CodeQL and Semgrep include common vulnerability rules for C/C++ and workflows.
static_analysis_fixed_justification: No confirmed medium-or-higher exploitable static-analysis findings are known open.
static_analysis_justification: CI runs CodeQL, Semgrep, clang-tidy, cppcheck, scan-build, GCC fanalyzer, IWYU, lizard, workflow linting, and local preflight guardrails.
static_analysis_often_justification: Static analysis runs in GitHub Actions on pull requests, pushes, and scheduled guardrail workflows.
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: GitHub Actions run tests and quality gates on PRs and pushes: https://github.com/arancormonk/dsd-neo/actions.
test_invocation_justification: Run cmake --preset dev-debug, cmake --build --preset dev-debug -j, and ctest --preset dev-debug --output-on-failure.
test_justification: CTest suite and invocation are documented in README.md and docs/testing.md.
test_most_justification: CTest suite covers the primary runtime, platform, DSP, IO, engine, FEC, crypto, protocol, core, and UI modules; no numeric coverage claim is made without a current coverage artifact.
test_policy_justification: docs/testing.md and CONTRIBUTING.md require tests for major new functionality.
tests_are_added_justification: Recent branch history includes focused tests for UI, runtime, protocol, DSP, and CodeQL/security cleanup changes.
tests_documented_added_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents test requirements for change proposals.
version_semver_justification: Release tags use SemVer-style vX.Y.Z identifiers.
version_unique_justification: Releases use unique vX.Y.Z tags at https://github.com/arancormonk/dsd-neo/releases.
vulnerabilities_critical_fixed_justifica: SECURITY.md requires rapid handling of critical vulnerabilities; none are currently known open.
vulnerabilities_fixed_60_days_justificat: No publicly known unpatched medium-or-higher vulnerabilities are currently known for this project.
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: SECURITY.md documents private vulnerability reporting via GitHub Security Advisories. Private reports are submitted at https://github.com/arancormonk/dsd-neo/security/advisories/new.
vulnerability_report_response_justificat: No vulnerability reports are known in the last six months; SECURITY.md documents response expectations.
warnings_fixed_justification: Required CI and pre-push checks include warnings-as-errors where configured.
warnings_justification: CI and hooks run compiler warnings, warnings-as-errors, clang-tidy, cppcheck, scan-build, GCC fanalyzer, IWYU, Semgrep, and lizard.
warnings_strict_justification: Guardrails include warnings-as-errors, strict clang-tidy, strict cppcheck, strict Semgrep, and static-analysis gates.
